publications.ref: Zhang B, Pirmoradian M, Chernobrovkin A, Zubarev RA. DeMix Workflow for Efficient Identification of Co-fragmented Peptides in High Resolution Data-dependent Tandem Mass Spectrometry. Mol Cell Proteomics. 2014 Aug 6. pii: mcp.O114.038877

STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: This is a human proteomics study using data-dependent acquisition shotgun proteomics for protein extract analysis. PRIDE database suggests HeLa cells were used, but this needs methods section confirmation. The study appears to be label-free based on no mention of chemical labeling. Key focus is on methodology development (DeMix workflow) rather than biological treatment comparisons.

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: Proteins (80 μg) were digested with trypsin and incubated at 37 °C for 9 h.

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: protein extracts were reduced and alkylated via incubation with 10 mm of DTT and iodoacetamide, respectively.

Characteristics[AlkylationReagent].evidence: protein extracts were reduced and alkylated via incubation with 10 mm of DTT and iodoacetamide, respectively.

Characteristics[Label].evidence: No chemical labeling methods mentioned in sample preparation protocol, and LC-MS/MS described as direct analysis without labeling reagents.

Characteristics[Modification].1.evidence: Variable Modifications = [oxidation of M, acetylation of protein N terminus]

Characteristics[Modification].2.evidence: Variable Modifications = [oxidation of M, acetylation of protein N terminus]

Comment[Separation].evidence: Thermo Scientific EASY-Spray columns (PepMap® RSLC, C18, 100 Å, 2 μm bead packed 50 cm column)

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: Sample preparation details are fully characterized. HeLa cells were processed using standard proteomics workflow with trypsin digestion, DTT reduction, and iodoacetamide alkylation. The study used label-free quantification with reverse phase LC separation. Three modifications were searched: carbamidomethylation (fixed) and oxidation/N-terminal acetylation (variable). No enrichment or fractionation methods were employed.

Comment[Instrument].evidence: Mass spectra were acquired with an Orbitrap Q Exactive mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific)

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: Peptide fragmentation was performed via higher-energy collisional dissociation

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: Mass spectra were acquired with an Orbitrap Q Exactive mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific) in a data-dependent manner using a top-20 method

Comment[CollisionEnergy].evidence: higher-energy collisional dissociation set at 25 V of normalized collisional energy

Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance].evidence: The second pass database search was performed on deconvoluted MS/MS data with a narrower MS tolerance (10 ppm)

Comment[FragmentMassTolerance].evidence: the tolerance for MS/MS was still 20 ppm

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: eluted in a 150 min LC-MS/MS experiment

Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].evidence: at a flow rate of 250 nL/min

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: Mass spectrometry instrumentation and acquisition parameters are fully characterized. The study used an Orbitrap Q Exactive with data-dependent acquisition (top-20 method), HCD fragmentation at 25V, and nanoESI ionization. Search parameters included 10 ppm precursor tolerance, 20 ppm fragment tolerance, and maximum 3 missed cleavages. LC conditions were 150 min gradient at 250 nL/min flow rate. All technical parameters for mass spectrometry analysis are now complete.

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.evidence: Three separate cell pellets were lysed

Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.evidence: For testing precursor selectivity, replicated experiments were performed with four different isolation widths: ±1.0, ±2.0, ±3.0, and ±4.0 Th

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples].evidence: Total raw files present: 7 files (3 biological replicates + 4 technical replicates with different isolation widths)

STEP4_RAW_FILES.summary: Study confirmed to use 3 biological replicates (HELA1, HELA2, HELA3) with 4 different isolation width conditions (±1.0, ±2.0, ±3.0, ±4.0 Th) totaling 7 raw files. The experimental design focuses on testing precursor selectivity with different isolation widths rather than biological treatments. Clear biological replicate structure identified from filename tokens and manuscript text.
